scope.summary: Define ElasticInsert and BindingTape as specialized edge-bound trim types in GarmentSpec: ElasticInsert applies a stiffness-and-gather override to a seam edge (compliance + gather_ratio from a material preset); BindingTape adds a thin rigid strip mesh along a panel edge with its own mass and stiffness; author the corresponding tailor_edge_trims migration and wire both into the XPBD substep as tack+stiffness overrides on the edge particle range
acceptance_criteria: ElasticInsert is a GarmentSpec trim_placement variant that targets a seam edge range, overriding that edge's gather_ratio and seam constraint compliance independently of the panel's base fabric preset
BindingTape generates a strip of GpuTrimBody geometry along the panel edge with configurable mass_per_mm and stiffness, participating in tack constraints at regular intervals along the edge
Both variants are stored in tailor_trim_placements with trim_category='elastic_insert' and 'binding_tape' respectively, extending the CHECK domain (migration required)
A waistband with ElasticInsert produces measurably tighter gather in simulation than the same seam with gather_ratio only (no elastic), verified in the integration test
BindingTape on a jacket hem prevents the hem from curling (stiffness effect) compared to an unsupported hem in the same drape
scope.risk_if_missed: Garments with elasticated waistbands, cuffs, and bound edges (a dominant construction technique in commercial ready-to-wear) cannot be simulated with correct material behavior; the operator's explicit inclusion of 'elastic, binding/tape' in the area scope is unaddressed; production garment simulations will show incorrect hang and gather at all elasticated or bound edges

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
